title: boehmeria nivea subsp. nipononivea
intro: Boehmeria nivea subsp. nipononivea, also known as Japanese paper mulberry, is a subspecies of Boehmeria nivea, a plant native to East Asia. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Boehmeria nivea subsp. nipononivea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine in the form of dried leaves or roots.
